body.title: [sprint] Define the node-activation embedding representation + LIKE=node-overlap
body.intention: I intend that 'embedding' in this system means a set of activated concept-nodes, so similarity is node-overlap and embeddings are computable in graph trees without an LLM.
body.description:
**Problem:** The whole epic rests on a concrete definition of the node-activation representation. DESIGN-FIRST (needs CC, not Haiku yet).
**Proposed shape:** Define: (a) text -> activated word-graph concept-node set (the embedding); how activation is computed/scored; (b) LIKE/similarity = node-overlap metric (Jaccard / weighted overlap / path-distance) — pick and justify; (c) how this replaces every dense-vector use (valence anchors, kNN) in node-overlap terms; (d) the on-node storage shape.
**OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS:** activation scoring; overlap metric choice; how kNN/valence arithmetic map to overlap. Resolve with Akien before implementation tickets unblock.
**Affected files:** design doc -> devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/; references word_graph.py.
**Design rules:** node-activation not dense vectors; living-entities node discipline.
**Scope boundary:** representation + metric definition only; no production code.
**Completion criteria:** a written representation+metric spec sufficient for a builder to implement the embed primitive; gates T-graph-embed-primitive.
**Test plan:** none (design); proof = the spec + Akien sign-off.
